entity: Arlington House, The Robert E. Lee Memorial
surface_forms: the house open to the public
aliases: Arlington House, The Robert E Lee Memorial
description: historic Custis family mansion operated by the U.S. National Park Service, located within Arlington National Cemetery
description_wiki: Arlington House is the historic Custis family mansion built by George Washington Parke Custis from 1803–1818 as a memorial to George Washington.

description: former skyscraper complex in Manhattan, New York (1968-2001)
description_wiki: The original World Trade Center (WTC) was a complex of seven buildings in the Financial District of Lower Manhattan in New York City.

aliases: Headquarters of the United States Department of Defense

Headquarters of the United States Department of War (1789–1947)
description: headquarters of the United States Department of Defense; named after the shape of the building, a polygon with five (greek: penta) sides
description_wiki: The Pentagon is the headquarters of the United States Department of Defense, in Arlington County, Virginia, across the Potomac River from Washington, D.C. The building was constructed on an accelerated schedule during World War II. As a symbol of the U.S. military, the phrase The Pentagon is often used as a metonym for the Department of Defense and its leadership.

surface_forms: the attacks on the World Trade Center
description_wiki: The September 11 attacks, colloquially known as 9/11, were a coordinated series of suicide attacks perpetrated by the Islamist terrorist organization al-Qaeda against the United States in 2001.

surface_forms: what used to be Arlington Hospital
description: healthcare organization in Arlington, United States
place in Virginia listed on National Register of Historic Places
description_wiki: Fort Myer is the previous name used for a U.S. Army post next to Arlington National Cemetery in Arlington County, Virginia, and across the Potomac River from Washington, D.C. Founded during the American Civil War as Fort Cass and Fort Whipple, the post merged in 2005 with the neighboring Marine Corps installation, Henderson Hall, and is today named Joint Base Myer–Henderson Hall.

description: 7,142-acre parkway in Virginia and the District of Columbia in the United States, that is maintained by the National Park Service
section of U.S. Numbered Highway in Virginia, United States
description_wiki: U.S. Route 50 (US 50) is a transcontinental highway which stretches from Ocean City, Maryland to West Sacramento, California.

description: city in the state of Virginia, United States
description_wiki: Alexandria is an independent city in the U.S. state of Virginia.

description: airport in Arlington County, Virginia, near Washington, D.C., United States
description_wiki: Ronald Reagan Washington National Airport (IATA: DCA, ICAO: KDCA, FAA LID: DCA) is a public airport in Arlington County, Virginia, United States, five miles (eight kilometers; four nautical miles) from Washington, D.C. The closest airport to the nation's capital, it is one of two airports owned by the federal government and operated by the Metropolitan Washington Airports Authority (MWAA) that serve the Washington metropolitan area; the other is Dulles International Airport (IAD), located about 25 miles (40 km; 22 nmi) to the west in Fairfax and Loudoun counties.
The United States Park Police (USPP) is the oldest uniformed federal law enforcement agency in the United States.
